get.summary: Get a list of user alerts
get.description: Get a list of user alerts.

parameters.name: cutoff
parameters.description: Unix timestamp of oldest alert to include. Note that unread or unviewed alerts are always included.

parameters.name: unviewed
parameters.description: If true, gets only unviewed alerts. Unviewed alerts have not been seen (in the standard UI).

parameters.name: unread
parameters.description: If true, gets only unread alerts. Unread alerts may have been seen but the content they relate to has not been viewed.

post.summary: Send an alert to the specified user
post.description: Send an alert to the specified user. Only available to super user keys.

to_user_id.description: ID of the user to receive the alert

alert.description: Text of the alert. May use the placeholder "{link}" to have the link automatically inserted.

from_user_id.description: If provided, the user to send the alert from. Otherwise, uses the current API user. May be 0 for an anonymous alert.

link_url.description: URL user will be taken to when the alert is clicked.

link_title.description: Text of the link URL that will be displayed. If no placeholder is present in the alert, will be automatically appended.

post.summary: Mark all alerts as read or viewed
post.description: Mark all alerts as read or viewed. Must specify "read" or "viewed" parameters.

read.description: If specified, marks all alerts as read.

viewed.description: If specified, marks all alerts as viewed. This will remove the alert counter but keep unactioned alerts highlighted.

get.summary: Get information about the specified alert
get.description: Get information about the specified alert.

post.summary: Mark the alert as viewed, read, or unread
post.description: Mark the alert as viewed, read, or unread. Marking as unviewed is not supported.

read.description: If specified, marks the alert as read.

unread.description: If specified, marks the alert as unread.

viewed.description: If specified, marks all alerts as viewed.

get.summary: Get attachments by API attachment key
get.description: Get attachments by API attachment key. Only returns attachments that have not been associated with content.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The API attachment key

post.description: Upload an attachment. An API attachment key must be created first. Must be submitted using multipart/form-data encoding.

key.description: The API attachment key to associated with

attachment.description: The attachment record of the successful upload

default.description:
Triggered if the user making the request does not match the user that created the attachment key.

**Possible errors**
- `attachment_key_user_wrong`: Triggered if the user making the request does not match the user that created the attachment key.

post.description: Create an attachment key. Allows attachments to be uploaded separately from the related content.

type.description: The content type of the attachment. Default types include post, conversation_message. Add-ons may add more.

context.description: Key-value pairs representing the context of the attachment. This will vary depending on content type and the action being taken. See relevant actions for further details.

attachment.description: The first attachment to be associated with the new key.

key.description: The attachment key created. This should be used to upload additional files or to associate uploaded attachments with other content.

attachment.description: If a file was provided and the upload was successful, this will describe the new attachment.

get.summary: Get information about the specified attachment
get.description: Get information about the specified attachment.

delete.description: Delete the specified attachment.

get.summary: Get the data that makes up the specified attachment
get.description: Get the data that makes up the specified attachment. The output is the raw binary data.

schema.description: The binary data is output directly, not JSON.

get.summary: Get an attachment thumbnail URL
get.description: Get an attachment thumbnail URL. URL returned via a 301 redirect.

url.description: The URL to the thumbnail is returned via a 301 redirect's Location header.

default.description:
Not found if the attachment does not have a thumbnail

**Possible errors**
- `not_found`: Not found if the attachment does not have a thumbnail

get.summary: Get an attachment thumbnail URL
get.description: Get an attachment thumbnail URL. URL returned via a 301 redirect.

url.description: The URL to the thumbnail is returned via a 301 redirect's Location header.

default.description:
Not found if the attachment does not have a thumbnail

**Possible errors**
- `not_found`: Not found if the attachment does not have a thumbnail

post.summary: Test a login and password for validity
post.description: Test a login and password for validity. Only available to super user keys. We strongly recommend the login and password parameters are passed into the request body rather than the query string.

login.description: The username or email address of the user to test

limit_ip.description: The IP that should be considered to be making the request. If provided, this will be used to prevent brute force attempts.

user.description: If successful, the user record of the matching user

post.summary: Look up an active user from session data
post.description: Look up an active user from session data. This can be used to help with seamless SSO with XF, assuming the session or remember cookies are available to your page. At least one of session_id and remember_cookie must be provided. Only available to super user keys.

session_id.description: If provided, checks for an active session with that ID.

remember_cookie.description: If provided, checks to see if this is an active "remember me" cookie value.

success.description: If false, no session or remember cookie could be found

user.description: If successful, the user record of the matching user. May be a guest.

post.description: Generate a login token. If the visitor is already logged into a XenForo account, they will not be logged into the specified account. Only available to super user keys.

limit_ip.description: If provided, locks the token to the specified IP for additional security

return_url.description: If provided, after logging the user will be returned to this URL. Otherwise they'll go to the XenForo index.

force.description: If provided, the login URL will forcibly replace the currently logged in user if a user is already logged in and different to the currently logged in user. Defaults to false.

remember.description: Controls whether the a "remember me" cookie will be set when the user logs in. Defaults to true.

login_url.description: Direct user to this URL to trigger a login

expiry_date.description: Unix timestamp of when the token expires. An error will be displayed if the token is expired or invalid

post.description: Reply to a conversation.

attachment_key.description: API attachment key to upload files. Attachment key content type must be conversation_message with context[conversation_id] set to this conversation ID.

get.summary: Get the specified conversation message
get.description: Get the specified conversation message.

post.description: Update the specified conversation message.

attachment_key.description: API attachment key to upload files. Attachment key content type must be conversation_message with context[message_id] set to this message ID.

post.summary: React to the specified conversation message
post.description: React to the specified conversation message.

reaction_id.description: ID of the reaction to use. Use the current reaction ID to undo.

action.description: "insert" or "delete" based on whether the reaction was added or removed.

get.summary: Get a list of user conversations
get.description: Get a list of user conversations.

parameters.name: starred
parameters.description: Only gets starred conversations if specified

parameters.name: unread
parameters.description: Only gets unread conversations if specified

post.description: Create a conversation.

recipient_ids.description: List of user IDs to send the conversation to

attachment_key.description: API attachment key to upload files. Attachment key content type must be conversation_message with no context.

conversation_open.description: If false, no replies may be made to this conversation.

open_invite.description: If true, any member of the conversation may add others

get.summary: Get information about the specified conversation
get.description: Get information about the specified conversation.

parameters.name: with_messages
parameters.description: If specified, the response will include a page of messages.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page of messages to include

messages.description: List of messages on the requested page

post.description: Update the specified conversation.

open_invite.description: If true, any member of the conversation can add others

conversation_open.description: If false, no further replies are allowed.

delete.summary: Delete a conversation from the user list
delete.description: Delete a conversation from the user list. Does not delete the conversation for other receivers.

parameters.name: ignore
parameters.description: If true, further replies to this conversation will be ignored. (Otherwise, replies will restore this conversation to the list.)

post.summary: Invite the specified users to this conversation
post.description: Invite the specified users to this conversation.

recipient_ids.description: List of user IDs to invite

post.summary: Set labels on the specified conversation
post.description: Set labels on the specified conversation.
Mark a conversation as read. This cannot move the

date.description: Unix timestamp to mark the conversation read to. If not specified, defaults to the current time.

post.description: Mark the conversation as unread. This marks all messages in the conversation as unread.

get.summary: Get a page of messages in the specified conversation
get.description: Get a page of messages in the specified conversation.

messages.description: List of messages on the requested page

post.summary: Set the star status of the specified conversation
post.description: Set the star status of the specified conversation.

star.description: If provided, sets the star status as specified. If not provided, toggles the status.

get.summary: Get a list of featured content
get.description: Get a list of featured content.

parameters.name: content_type
parameters.description: A content type to restrict results to

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: A user ID to restrict results to

get.summary: Get information about the specified forum
get.description: Get information about the specified forum.

parameters.name: with_threads
parameters.description: If true, gets a page of threads in this forum

parameters.name: prefix_id
parameters.description: Filters to only threads with the specified prefix.

parameters.name: starter_id
parameters.description: Filters to only threads started by the specified user ID.

parameters.name: last_days
parameters.description: Filters to threads that have had a reply in the last X days.

parameters.name: unread
parameters.description: Filters to unread threads only. Ignored for guests.

parameters.name: thread_type
parameters.description: Filters to threads of the specified thread type.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: Method of ordering: last_post_date, post_date. When in a specific forum context: title, reply_count, view_count, vote_score, first_post_reaction_score.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Either "asc" or "desc" for ascending or descending. Applies only if an order is provided.

sticky.description: If on page 1, a list of sticky threads in this forum. Does not count towards the per page limit.

post.description: Mark a forum as read. This cannot mark a forum as unread or

date.description: Unix timestamp to mark the forum read to. If not specified, defaults to the current time.

get.summary: Get a page of threads from the specified forum
get.description: Get a page of threads from the specified forum.

parameters.name: prefix_id
parameters.description: Filters to only threads with the specified prefix.

parameters.name: starter_id
parameters.description: Filters to only threads started by the specified user ID.

parameters.name: last_days
parameters.description: Filters to threads that have had a reply in the last X days.

parameters.name: unread
parameters.description: Filters to unread threads only. Ignored for guests.

parameters.name: thread_type
parameters.description: Filters to threads of the specified thread type.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: Method of ordering: last_post_date, post_date. When in a specific forum context: title, reply_count, view_count, vote_score, first_post_reaction_score.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Either "asc" or "desc" for ascending or descending. Applies only if an order is provided.

sticky.description: If on page 1, a list of sticky threads in this forum. Does not count towards the per page limit.

get.summary: Get site and API information
get.description: Get site and API information.

site_title.description: Title of the site this API relates to

base_url.description: The base URL of the XenForo install this API relates to

key[type].description: Type of the API key accessing the API (guest, user or super)

key[user_id].description: If a user key, the ID of the user the key is for; null otherwise

key[allow_all_scopes].description: If true, all scopes can be accessed

key[scopes].description: A list of scopes this key can access (if not allowed to access all scopes)

get.summary: Get information about the current user
get.description: Get information about the current user.

post.summary: Update information about the current user
post.description: Update information about the current user.
Update the current user avatar.

delete.description: Delete the current user avatar.

post.summary: Update the current user email address
post.description: Update the current user email address.

confirmation_required.description: True if email confirmation is required for this change

post.description: Update the current user password.

get.summary: Get a list of media items
get.description: Get a list of media items.

parameters.name: media_type
parameters.description: Filters to only media of the specified type. Valid types: image, audio, video, embed.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: Filters to only media created by the specified user ID.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: Method of ordering: media_date, comment_count, rating_weighted, reaction_score, view_count

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Either "asc" or "desc" for ascending or descending. Applies only if an order is provided.

post.description: Create a media item. Provide either album_id or category_id, and either embed_url or file.

album_id.description: ID of the album to add media to. Required if category_id is not provided.

category_id.description: ID of the category to add media to. Required if album_id is not provided.

embed_url.description: URL to embed. Required if file is not uploaded.

file.description: Media file to upload. Required if embed_url is not provided.

media.description:
If the media item was created immediately.

**Note** Conditionally returned

transcoding.description:
If true, the media requires transcoding and will be available later.

**Note** Conditionally returned

get.summary: Get information about the specified media item
get.description: Get information about the specified media item.

parameters.name: with_comments
parameters.description: If specified, the response will include a page of comments.

post.description: Update the specified media item.

delete.description: Delete the specified media item. Defaults to soft deletion.

get.summary: Get a page of comments on the specified media item
get.description: Get a page of comments on the specified media item.

get.summary: Get a media item file
get.description: Get a media item file.

post.summary: Set feature state for the specified media
post.description: Set feature state for the specified media.

title.description:
A custom title, if desired.

**Note** Conditionally returned

snippet.description:
A custom snippet, if desired.

**Note** Conditionally returned

image.description:
A custom image, if desired.

**Note** Conditionally returned

date.description:
A custom feature date, if desired.

**Note** Conditionally returned

unfeature_days.description:
A custom number of days after which to unfeature the content, if desired.

**Note** Conditionally returned

always_visible.description:
Whether or not the feature should be visible even when the visitor may not view the content.

**Note** Conditionally returned

post.summary: React to the specified media item
post.description: React to the specified media item.

reaction_id.description: ID of the reaction to use. Use the current reaction ID to undo.

action.description: "insert" or "delete" based on whether the reaction was added or removed.

post.summary: Delete the feature for the specified media
post.description: Delete the feature for the specified media.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Either "asc" or "desc" for ascending or descending. Applies only if an order is provided.

post.description: Create an album.

view_privacy.description: Privacy level for viewing: public, members, shared, private

view_user_ids.description: User IDs who can view when view_privacy is "shared"

add_privacy.description: Privacy level for adding media: public, members, shared, private

add_user_ids.description: User IDs who can add when add_privacy is "shared"

get.summary: Get information about the specified search forum
get.description: Get information about the specified search forum.

parameters.name: with_threads
parameters.description: If true, gets a page of threads in this search forum

threads.description: Threads on this page. Note: this will always respect viewing user permissions regardless of whether the API is set to bypass permissions.

sticky.description: If on page 1, a list of sticky threads in this forum. Does not count towards the per page limit.

get.summary: Get a page of threads from the specified search forum
get.description: Get a page of threads from the specified search forum.

threads.description: Threads on this page. Note: this will always respect viewing user permissions regardless of whether the API is set to bypass permissions.

sticky.description: If on page 1, a list of sticky threads in this forum. Does not count towards the per page limit.

get.summary: Get site statistics
get.description: Get site statistics.

get.summary: Get a list of threads
get.description: Get a list of threads.

parameters.name: prefix_id
parameters.description: Filters to only threads with the specified prefix.

parameters.name: starter_id
parameters.description: Filters to only threads started by the specified user ID.

parameters.name: last_days
parameters.description: Filters to threads that have had a reply in the last X days.

parameters.name: unread
parameters.description: Filters to unread threads only. Ignored for guests.

parameters.name: thread_type
parameters.description: Filters to threads of the specified thread type.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: Method of ordering: last_post_date, post_date. When in a specific forum context: title, reply_count, view_count, vote_score, first_post_reaction_score.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Either "asc" or "desc" for ascending or descending. Applies only if an order is provided.

post.description: Create a thread. Thread type data can be set using additional input specific to the target thread type.

node_id.description: ID of the forum to create the thread in.

message.description: Body of the first post in the thread.

discussion_type.description: The type of thread to create. Specific types may require additional input.

prefix_id.description: ID of the prefix to apply to the thread. If not valid in the selected forum, will be ignored.

tags.description: Array of tag names to apply to the thread.

custom_fields[<name>].description: Value to apply to the custom field with the specified name.

attachment_key.description: API attachment key to upload files. Attachment key context type must be post with context[node_id] set to the ID of the forum this is being posted in.

default.description:
No permission error.

**Possible errors**
- `no_permission`: No permission error.

get.summary: Get information about the specified thread
get.description: Get information about the specified thread.

parameters.name: with_posts
parameters.description: If specified, the response will include a page of posts.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page of posts to include

parameters.name: with_first_post
parameters.description: If specified, the response will contain the first post in the thread.

parameters.name: with_last_post
parameters.description: If specified, the response will contain the last post in the thread.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: Request a particular sort order for posts from the available options for the thread type

first_unread.description:
If the thread is unread, information about the first unread post.

**Note** Conditionally returned

first_post.description:
If requested, information about the first post in the thread.

**Note** Conditionally returned

last_post.description:
If requested, information about the last post in the thread.

**Note** Conditionally returned

pinned_post.description:
The pinned first post of the thread, if specified by the thread type.

**Note** Conditionally returned

highlighted_posts.description:
A list of highlighted posts, if relevant to the thread type. The reason for highlighting depends on thread type.

**Note** Conditionally returned

posts.description: List of posts on the requested page. Note that even if the first post is pinned, it will be included here.

post.description: Update the specified thread.

delete.description: Delete the specified thread. Defaults to soft deletion.

post.summary: Convert a thread to the specified type
post.description: Convert a thread to the specified type. Additional thread type data can be set using input specific to the new thread type.

post.summary: Set feature state for the specified thread
post.description: Set feature state for the specified thread.

title.description:
A custom title, if desired.

**Note** Conditionally returned

snippet.description:
A custom snippet, if desired.

**Note** Conditionally returned

image.description:
A custom image, if desired.

**Note** Conditionally returned

date.description:
A custom feature date, if desired.

**Note** Conditionally returned

unfeature_days.description:
A custom number of days after which to unfeature the content, if desired.

**Note** Conditionally returned

always_visible.description:
Whether or not the feature should be visible even when the visitor may not view the content.

**Note** Conditionally returned

post.description: Mark a thread as read. This cannot mark a thread as unread or

date.description: Unix timestamp to mark the thread read to. If not specified, defaults to the current time.

post.summary: Move the specified thread to a different forum
post.description: Move the specified thread to a different forum. Only simple title/prefix updates are supported at the same time.

prefix_id.description: If set, will update the thread's prefix. Prefix must be valid in the target forum.

title.description: If set, updates the thread's title

notify_watchers.description: If true, users watching the target forum will receive a notification as if this thread were created in the target forum

starter_alert.description: If true, the thread starter will receive an alert notifying them of the move

starter_alert_reason.description: The reason for the move to include with the thread starter alert

highlighted_posts.description:

post.summary: Delete the feature for the specified thread

Vote on the specified thread. Applies only when voting is supported.

type.description: Type of vote, "up" or "down". Use the current type to undo.

action.description: "insert" or "delete" based on whether the reaction was added or removed.

get.summary: Get a list of users
get.description: Get a list of users. Results are ordered alphabetically.

post.description: Create a user.

username_change_visible.description: If true and the username is changed, the change will be visible (shown in the public list of previous usernames).

get.summary: Find users by email
get.description: Find users by email. Only available to admin users. Also available when bypassing permissions.

user.description: The user that matched the given email exactly

get.summary: Find users by a prefix of their user name
get.description: Find users by a prefix of their user name.

exact.description: The user that matched the given username exactly

recommendations.description: A list of users that match the prefix of the username (but not exactly)

XF-Api-User.name: XF-Api-User
XF-Api-User.description: User ID to execute the request as. Requires a super user API key.

api_bypass_permissions.name: api_bypass_permissions
api_bypass_permissions.description: Whether or not to bypass the context user permissions. Requires a super user API key.

XF-Used-Api-Version.description: The API version used to render the response.

XF-Request-User.description: The user ID of the request user.

XF-Request-User-Extras.description: Additional information about the request user in JSON encoding. Requires XF-Request-User header to be non-zero.

scopes.alert:read: Covers viewing the active user's alerts. Also includes read/unread marking of these alerts.
scopes.alert:write: Covers creating custom user alerts. Applies to super-user keys only.
scopes.conversation:write: Covers creating or updating direct messages.
scopes.media:delete_hard: Covers hard-deleting media, albums and their comments.
scopes.media:read: Covers viewing media, albums, and their comments.
scopes.media:write: Covers creating, updating and soft-deleting media, albums and their comments.
scopes.media_category:read: Covers view media categories or the list of categories.
scopes.media_category:write: Covers updating or creating a media category.
scopes.node:read: Covers reading the list of nodes or an individual node.
scopes.node:write: Covers creating or updating a node. Node deletion is not included.
scopes.profile_post:delete_hard: Covers hard-deleting profile posts and profile post comments.
scopes.profile_post:read: Covers viewing profile posts and profile post comments.
scopes.profile_post:write: Covers creating, updating and soft-deleting profile posts and profile post comments.
scopes.resource:read: Covers viewing resources and their content.
scopes.resource:write: Covers creating, updating and soft-deleting resources and their content.
scopes.resource_category:read: Covers viewing resource categories or the list of categories.
scopes.resource_category:write: Covers updating or creating a resource category.
scopes.resource_rating:write: Covers creating, updating and soft-deleting resource ratings/reviews.
scopes.thread:read: Covers reading thread and post information.
scopes.thread:write: Covers creating, updating and soft-deleting threads and posts.
scopes.user:read: Covers reading user profile information. Note that some profile details may be returned with other data.
